senses.definition: a person or thing that promotes or brings about growth or development; also a person who blooms or develops later in life
examples: An early bloom arrives in the garden, a true bloomer.
The new policy is a real bloomer for the local economy.

senses.definition: a fictional or obscure term related to bloom or flowering (not widely used in standard English)
examples: The manuscript mentions bloomeria as a field of study in botany folklore.
In some dialects, bloomeria refers to a place where flowers are cultivated.

senses.definition: plural of bloomeria; places or systems associated with bloom or flowering (rare/archaic usage)
examples: The bloomeries remained a mystery to most researchers.
Several bloomeries were cited in the old botanical atlas.

senses.definition: the act or condition of blooming; in some contexts, a figurative flourishing or flowering of something
examples: The bloomers of spring mark a period of natural bloomism.
Historically, bloomers rhetoric described a cultural bloomism.

senses.definition: loose-fitting pants gathered at the ankle, worn by women in the early 20th century; also a slang term for an embarrassing mistake
examples: She wore a pair of vintage bloomers in the theatre production.
In old slang, he called the blunder a real bloomer.

senses.definition: a furnace or kiln where iron ore is heated with charcoal to produce wrought iron
examples: The bloomery was used before blast furnaces became common.
Archaeologists found a bloomery near the old mining site.

senses.definition: a poetic or archaic term for a sudden fall in bloom or flowering
examples: The bloomfell of spring surprised the valley with sparse blossoms.
In literature, bloomfell imagery signals a passing season.

senses.definition: more in bloom; having more flowers or flourishing
examples: The garden looks bloomier after the rain.
This month is bloomier than last.

senses.definition: most in bloom; having the most flowers or flourishing
examples: The garden is the bloom-iest it has been all year.
It is the bloom-iest time of the season.

senses.definition: flowering or thriving; growing well
examples: The garden is blooming this spring.
We are blooming with energy after the break.

senses.definition: the act of flowering; a period of rapid growth or flourishing
examples: Spring is the blooming of the garden.
The city experienced a bloomings of cultural events.

senses.definition: in a blooming or flourishing manner; very successfully
examples: Business was bloomingingly profitable last quarter.
The garden grew bloomingly under her care.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being in bloom; level of flowering
examples: Gardeners judge bloomings by the bloomingness of the plants.
Seasonal bloominess varies by weather.

senses.definition: plural of blooming; periods or instances of flowering
examples: The bloomings in the garden were impressive this year.
Across the district, several bloomings occurred in spring.

senses.definition: a poetic or whimsical term for a person who is related to or embodies bloom or youth
examples: The festival celebrated the bloomkin of the village's gardens.
Children were called bloomkin in the old ballad.

senses.definition: not in bloom; lacking flowers or growth
examples: The winter garden was bloomless.
A bloomless hedge looked bare after frost.

senses.definition: flowers on a plant or tree; the act of flowering
examples: The cherry trees are heavy with blooms.
Blooms of the garden show their colors in spring.

senses.definition: a person from or associated with Bloomsbury, a district in London known for literary and intellectual culture
examples: Bloomsburian writers gathered in the 20th century.
As a Bloomsburian, she valued quiet, scholarly living.

senses.definition: a district in central London known for its literary and artistic associations; also a university press and neighborhood name
examples: Bloomsbury is famous for its literary history.
We visited Bloomsbury to see the British Museum and universities nearby.

senses.definition: covered with or smelling like flowers; cheerful or optimistic
examples: The garden looked bloom y and bright after rain.
She wore a bloom y dress that matched the spring mood.

senses.definition: a short, sudden, popping or bubbling sound; a minor or humorous mistake when recording or streaming
examples: I heard a loud bloop from the speaker.
The video glitch was just a bloop.

senses.definition: to produce a bloop sound or to fail briefly with a minor glitch
examples: The mic blooped during the livestream.
If the system bloops, try restarting.

senses.definition: past tense of bloop; produced a bloop sound or experienced a minor glitch
examples: The recording blooped twice and ruined the take.
It blooped unexpectedly during the demo.

senses.definition: a funny mistake, especially during filming or recording
examples: The director laughed at the blooper reel.
She saved the blooper for the end of the show.

senses.definition: plural of blooper; funny mistakes caught during filming or recording
examples: The bloopers after the credits kept the audience laughing.
They watched bloopers from the comedy show.

senses.definition: more blooper-like or more prone to mistakes; more ridiculous
examples: That bloopier scene made the crowd howl.
The bloopier take was kept for the blooper reel.

senses.definition: most blooper-like or most ridiculous
examples: The bloopiest moment of the film had everyone laughing.
Her bloopiest line was left in as a joke.

senses.definition: the act of flowering; a period of growth
examples: The blooming of the cherry trees is beautiful.
Spring brings a time of blooming around the city.

senses.definition: in a blooming or flourishing way
examples: The market was bloomingly lively this weekend.
Her ideas were bloomingly creative.

senses.definition: the state or quality of blooming; degree of flowering
examples: Gardeners assess bloomingness to plan care.
Climate affects bloomingness year to year.

senses.definition: plural of blooming; multiple periods or instances of flowering
examples: The garden had several bloomings throughout the season.
Different plants show bloomings at different times.

senses.definition: a playful or affectionate term for a person associated with bloom or youth
examples: The festival welcomed every bloomkin with smiles.
Children were called bloomkins in the story.

senses.definition: without flowers or growth; lacking vitality
examples: The winter landscape was bloomless.
A bloomless garden can feel sad in the cold months.

senses.definition: see Bloomsbury; often used as a proper noun referring to the London district or associated groups
examples: Bloomsbury is known for its literary heritage.
The Bloomsbury Group influenced 20th-century art and thought.

senses.definition: an area of central London; also a publishing house and educational associations
examples: The bookstore in Bloomsbury sells academic texts.
Bloomsbury is a hub for scholars and writers.

senses.definition: covered with blooms or blossoms; flowery in style
examples: The garden looked bloom y with roses.
Her dress was bloom y with bright patterns.

senses.definition: a short, hollow sound or error during recording; see blooper
examples: A tiny bloop interrupted the podcast.
The bloop was caught on the audio track.

senses.definition: past tense of bloop; produced a bloop sound or glitch
examples: The recording blooped briefly and audio was muted.
It blooped twice during the stream.

senses.definition: a funny mistake, especially in film or television
examples: The blooper reel had everyone laughing.
Watch the blooper at the end of the episode.

senses.definition: plural of blooper; funny mistakes broadcast or filmed
examples: The bloopers from the show are posted online.
They shared several bloopers during the credits.

senses.definition: more blooper-like; more prone to mistakes
examples: The bloopier take was kept for the blooper reel.
That scene is bloomier than the previous one.

senses.definition: most blooper-like; most ridiculous or funny
examples: That was the bloopiest moment of the show.
They premiered the bloopiest clips at the end.
